entity.summary: Matrixport-launched centralized cryptocurrency exchange that began operating in August 2020 and offered spot, futures, perpetuals, options, mining, and financial products. Bit.com announced an orderly business wind-down on December 27, 2025, stopped all trading by January 31, 2026, moved remaining balances to a withdrawal-only backup system, and set March 31, 2026 as the final service and withdrawal deadline. The bit.com domain now presents the broader BIT financial-services group, formerly Matrixport, rather than the former retail exchange.
entity.notes: HEI collapses the source spot and futures rows into one historical exchange entity. The terminal date is 2026-03-31, when the published migration and backup-withdrawal window ended; core trading had already stopped on 2026-01-31. The current BIT wealth, OTC, custody, RWA, and securities services are not treated as continued operation of the former Bit.com crypto exchange.

events.title: Bit.com Exchange launched
events.description: Matrixport launched Bit.com as a cryptocurrency derivatives exchange in August 2020, later expanding it into a full-suite spot and derivatives venue.
events.notes: Month-level marker from first-party Bit.com launch and operating-history material.

events.title: Bit.com announced a phased exchange shutdown
events.description: Bit.com announced a business restructuring and user-asset migration plan that immediately stopped new registrations, restricted derivatives, and scheduled the end of spot trading and remaining services.
events.notes: The announcement created a staged wind-down rather than an immediate terminal state.

events.title: Bit.com Exchange completed its shutdown
events.description: After all trading ended on January 31 and remaining assets moved to a withdrawal-only backup system, Bit.com's final service and withdrawal deadline expired on March 31, 2026.
events.notes: The current bit.com domain belongs to the rebranded BIT financial-services group and does not represent continuity of the closed exchange.

evidence.title: What is Bit.com
evidence.notes: First-party introduction identifies Bit.com as a Matrixport-launched derivatives exchange in August 2020.

evidence.title: Bit.com mobile fixed-savings product and exchange history
evidence.notes: First-party operating history states that Bit.com had been online since August 2020.

evidence.title: Follow-up Announcement on Business Adjustment and User Asset Migration
evidence.notes: First-party notice specifies the January 31 main-site and trading cutoff, backup withdrawal system, and migration arrangements.

evidence.title: Crypto exchange Bit.com set to wind down operations
evidence.notes: Independent report preserves the phased shutdown timeline and user-asset migration plan.

evidence.title: Analyst-loved crypto exchange announces unexpected shutdown
evidence.notes: Independent reporting documents the March 31 final deadline and distinguishes the exchange closure from Matrixport's broader services.

evidence.title: Matrixport Review: Bit.com Closed 2026, Platform Still Active
evidence.notes: Post-wind-down review distinguishes the closed Bit.com exchange from the still-operating Matrixport/BIT financial platform.

evidence.title: Matrixport Rebrands as BIT
evidence.notes: First-party statement confirms that the current bit.com identity is the rebranded Matrixport financial-services group, supporting repurposed-domain treatment for the historical exchange record.
